title: Is anyone embedding boards into their own site portals?
excerpt:
I’m wondering if anyone has successfully created portals and embedded customer boards into their site. 
I have an idea of instead of giving my customers access to a shared board, we create a portal on our site via Wordpr…

posts.author: bysam (SB)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m wondering if anyone has successfully created portals and embedded customer boards into their site.

I have an idea of instead of giving my customers access to a shared board, we create a portal on our site via Wordpress plugins, and when a customer logs in to the portal, they can have access to their board.

Is anyone doing this? If so, could you please explain how you have it setup and how you are using it?

posts.author: JPinBe (John Pescod)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @bysam. Only yesterday I asked about this. See my post Showing board items status dynamically in a connected website

posts.author: robertogentile (Roberto Gentile - Inova e-Business)
posts.cookedHtml: @bysam Yes! I’ve a programmer that did some feature for my company. Please tell him: caiocsl@gmail.com . He write and speak in english and I believe he will help you.

posts.author: bysam (SB)
posts.cookedHtml: I was more curious as to how people are already using it. Not ready to have something built, just want to understand the current use cases.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: We only use embedded board views for sharing information that the viewer doesn’t need to interact with at all. If a client needs to be able to reply, comment, filter, etc. then we make them Guests on the board and they sign in to see their board(s).

posts.author: bysam (SB)
posts.cookedHtml:
@PolishedGeek having looked at your company, it looks like you build some custom apps for customers using Monday.com. Is there a reason you don’t internally create customer portals using API’s, for your clients to interact with so Monday stays only as a backend tool?

Was that something you’ve ever considered internally?

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @bysam -

We never considered building out our own frontend portal on top of monday․com. While I’m sure there are some use cases where it makes sense, for us it didn’t. It was so absurdly easy to onboard our clients onto monday․com, plus there’s an excellent mobile app and tons of new features added every month. Building monday․com apps gives us the power to add unique views and features while still taking full advantage of every native monday․com feature.

I’d be happy to chat with you about why we went in this direction. We used to have a separate internal system (JIRA), but not anymore. Feel free to reserve a time on our calendar:

meet.polishedgeek.com

Book a call with Polished Geek: Do more with monday․com

Book a call with a Polished Geek monday․com certified consultant.

posts.author: tysidaho (Ty Palmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
We use Tovuti as a portal for car dealers that we partner with to provide auto loans. We use a form that the dealers will out to submit a deal, and we track it all along the way through to funding in a single pulse/item in Monday. We have “views” set up for each dealership where the view only displays certain columns, and we’ve set that view to be public, but have iframed it into a window that opens with a link in each dealerships portal in Tovuti. That way the dealerships can easily see what documents we still need to fund their deal, and if it has been funded. I’ll include a screenshot with our customer’s names redacted.

MondaySample1456×848 150 KB

posts.author: robgibbs (Rob Gibbs)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a customer portal that manages a lot of documents and document metadata (contract related dates, etc…) It is in SharePoint. We have a separate set of pages for projects and we surface timelines/Gannt charts and boards for executive access to project progress. We’d really like to be able to surface dashboards, but you can’t do that (yet? Monday?) This way Execs only have one location to go for all interactions with our company.

posts.author: kstatz (Kate)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Rob,

I am embedding individual charts into our portal and it works pretty well allowing our Leadership to view in one place/one page. It works pretty well until yesterday.

Yesterday the data began to not pull over. The embedded charts are there but the data all shows 1’s.

It looks like you posted here awhile ago. I’d love to hear if you were able to embed into your portal and if it’s working for you.

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @kstatz

If you’re having issues with the embedded view for your charts we’d love to have our support team take a closer look.
You can contact them at support@monday.com

They’ll be able to work with you on a 1:1 basis to see why this might not be working as expected for you!

Best,

Dani
